title: قرار الرئيس الاعلى للجامعة رقم 197 لسنة 2003: في شأن إنشاء برنامج الدراسات العليا للحصول على درجة ماجستير الاستشعار عن بعد ونظم المعلومات الجغرافية

provisions.title: انتهاءفي 24/11/2005
provisions.content:
مكرر)من اللائحةالتنفيذيةلقانون إنشاءوتنظيمالجامعةبإضافة فقرةرابعة إليها،لتصبح هذهالمادة كمايلي:اوليفيااستلمت في 23/11/2005تصحيحانتهاءفي 24/11/20051–يمنح مجلسالجامعة بناءعلى اقتراحمجلس كليةالعلوم،وتوصية مجلسالشؤونالعلميةوالتعليمية،وبناء على عرضمدير الجامعةدرجةالماجستير فيعلوم البيئة،ويصدر بذلكقرار منالرئيسالاعلىللجامعة.2–يمنح مجلسالجامعة بناءعلى اقتراحعمادةالدراساتالعليابالجامعة،وتوصية مجلسالشؤونالعلميةوالتعليمية،وبناء على عرضمدير الجامعةدرجة ماجستيرالعلوم فيمواردالمياه،ويصدر بذلكقرار منالرئيسالاعلىللجامعة.3–يمنح مجلسالجامعة بناءعلى اقتراحعمادةالدراساتالعليابالجامعةوتوصية مجلسالشؤونالعلميةوالتعليمية،وبناء على عرضمدير الجامعةدرجةالماجستير فيعلوم وهندسةالمواد،ويصدر بذلكقرار منالرئيسالاعلىللجامعة.4–يمنح مجلسالجامعة بناءعلى اقتراحعمادةالدراساتالعليابالجامعةوتوصية مجلسالشؤونالعلميةوالتعليمية،وبناء على عرضمدير الجامعةدرجةالماجستير فيالاستشعار عنبعد ونظمالمعلوماتالجغرافية،ويصدر بذلكقرار منالرئيسالاعلىللجامعة.المادة3ينشرهذا القرار فيالجريدةالرسميةويعمل بهاعتبارا منبداية العامالجامعي 2004/2005م.المادة4علىالجهاتالمعنيةتنفيذ هذاالقرار كلفيما يخصه.صدرفي 15/10/1424هـ.
الموافق9/12/2003م.نهيانمبارك آلنهيانالرئيسالاعلىللجامعةنشرهذا القرار فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم406ص 424.UnitedArab Emirates UniversityProposal for the Programof: Master of Science inRemote Sensing and Geographic Information Systems October, 2003Summary of the Proposed Masterof Science in Remote Sensing and Geographic Information SystemsAdmissionRequirements- B.Sc.degree with an overall Grade Point Average (GPA) of 2.5 (C+) or better in theField of applied sciences and social sciences. Experienced users in the Field ofremote sensing and GIS are encouraged to apply even their GPA is less than 2,5.A limited number of students will be accepted based on preferences.-Proficiency in English is required according to the Graduate Studies guidelines,which requires a score of 550 in TOEFL, or IELTS score of 6, or 70% or higher inthe University English Examination administered by the University GeneralRequirements Unit (UGRU). Student scoring TOEFL between 450 and 550 would berequired to take 1-2 courses of technical English. Those who have studied theirB.Sc. in an English-based country can be exempted from the English requirements.- Passingan interview.-Employed candidates are required to submit a written permission to pursuegraduate studies from their employers.ParticipatingCollegesTheprogram gets its unique strength because of its interdisciplinary nature. Thecourses included in the program will be taught by Faculty members from thecolleges of Humanities and Social Sciences, Sciences, Engineering, InformationTechnology, and Business and Administration.Language:English will be used as a medium of instruction for the program.Modes ofEnrollment: Students may enroll as full- time or as part-time in a thesis ornon-thesis track.Duration:2 years for full-time and 3 to 4 years for part-time.Expectednumber of students: 10 per year.DegreeRequirementsThe M.Sc.in Remote sensing and GIS program consists of two options:Option I(with M.Sc. thesis): Student must complete 30 credit hours for graduation. Thisis divided among core courses (18 credit hours), elective courses (6 credithours), and M.Sc. thesis (6 credit hours).Option 2(Non-thesis): Student must complete 34 credit hours for graduation. This isdivided among core courses (18 credit hours), elective courses (12 credithours), and a capstone research project (4 credit hours).In bothoptions the majority of courses will be offered in evening, so working engineersand other professionals can attend.DegreeAwardedHavingsuccessfully completed the taught component, the award of the M.Sc. is based onsatisfactory performance in the courses and the thesis or research project.Students must achieve at least an overall GPA of 3.0 to graduate.PrefaceThisproposal is the second version of the document prepared by a special committeeassigned by the Dean of Graduate Studies at the UAE University in March 2002.The committee members (PhDs) are:NameCollegE-mailMobileAhmad MassasatiHumanities and SocialSciencesA.Massasati@uaeu.ac.ae5737787Ahmed El-MowafyEngineeringAhmed.mowafy@uaeu.ac.ae7535840NameCollegE-mailMobileAlaaeldin AlyInformationTechnologyaly@uaeu.ac.ae6425185Mohamed YagoubHumanities and SocialSciencesmyagoub@uaeu.ac.ae5438788Salem EssaSciencesSalem.essa@uaeu.ac.ae7132865Thecommittee had worked in coordination with:ErnestPeck, PhDAdvisorto the Vice Chancellor for Graduate StudiesPARTI: Definition of the ProgramI.I INTRODUCTIONTheimportance of the spatial dimension in examining issues such as regionaldevelopment, land use planning and environmental monitoring is widelyrecognized. In work of this kind, it is necessary to interrelate informationderived from sources such as censuses, maps, surveys, aerial photographs andsatellite imagery, each of which may utilize a variety of methods of spatialreferencing. The methods required for these types of investigation and theanalysis of the resulting databases, which link information to maps stored indigital form, constitute the field of Remote Sensing and GeographicalInformation Systems (GIS).Applicationof GIS in the UAE is of high demand in municipalities, telecommunication, water,electricity, oil, and coastal sectors. A survey conducted by one of the stafffrom the UAE University has shown that the need for GIS professionals in the UAEis between 1 to 15 persons per department (Appendix B).The UAEUniversity is well recognized as one of the leading centers in the area in thefield of Geographical Information Systems. GIS and related technologies(including remote sensing, photogrammetry, survey data acquisition and digitalmapping) are taught at faculties of Engineering, Humanities and Social Sciences,and Sciences since 1990s.Theproposed program gets its unique strength because of its interdisciplinarynature. The courses included in the program will be taught by staff from thefaculties of Humanities and Social Sciences, Sciences, Engineering, and Collegeof Information Technology. Available facilities at these faculties (Facultymembers, staff, computing, data, references, journals) provide the foundationfor practical and research applications. Members of staff maintain close links,in terms of research projects and consultancy, with a number of organizationswhich make extensive use of spatially referenced data. A wide variety ofsoftware for Remote Sensing and GIS applications is available, with closerelationships being maintained with vendors.1.2 RELEVANCE TO THEU.A.E.In theUAE, many organizations continue to experience rapid growth in the volumes ofspatial data. Unfortunately, severe shortages of skilled personnel, combinedwith a lack of understanding of the potential uses of GIS methods, mean thatthese same organizations have continuing difficulties in managing these complexproblems. Since the late 1980s interest in the field of GIS has been burgeoning,and the pace of new developments continues to grow.Theproposed M.Sc. in Remote Sensing and GIS at the UAE University will be the firsteducational programs of its kind in the area. The M.Sc. is designed to provide aunique educational opportunity in this subject area, and improve theunderstanding of the field of Remote Sensing and GIS, together with itsimplications and societal ratificationsTheprogram is expected to attract applicants from a variety of backgrounds,including recent graduates in the social, earth and environmental sciences,engineering, together with managers, researchers and technical specialists fromcommerce, industry and government organizations. As part of the educationalprocess, the program aims to bring together a diversity of individuals into eachcourse group, with their varied range of experiences. Course participants areexpected to come from the UAE, Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the Middle East,and other countries.1.3 OBJECTIVES OF THEPROGRAM- Toprovide an intensive educational opportunity designed to equip students foreither a career in the Remote Sensing and Geographical Information industry orfor further research.- Todevelop detailed knowledge and understanding of recent advances in RemoteSensing, GIS, computing technology, and software engineering methods that arerelevant to Remote Sensing and GIS.- Tobuild up a high level of practical skills and experience in the use of RemoteSensing and Geographical Information System tools.1.4 OTHER EDUCATIONALOBJECTIVES OF THE PROGRAM- Toachieved the program's vision, goals, and objectives, the following additionaleducational objectives are planned:- Toprovide students with opportunities throughout the curriculum to developcomputational techniques, and written and oral communication skills.- Toprovide students with the recognition and understanding of the professional andsocietal responsibilities associated with working in the industry, environment,and administration.- Toprovide students and staff with an environment in which they can address globaland regional environmental problems and investigate models of solution, sharpentheir aesthetic sense, and embrace their own social and personaldevelopment.1.5 PARTICIPATINGCOLLEGESTheprogram will be managed and coordinated by the Dean of Graduate Studies and anexecutive committee. The departments in the UAE that involved in Remote Sensingand GIS are diverse (refer to Appendix B). The interdisciplinary nature of theprogram requires candidates to work in association with and attend courses givenby a number of departments in the University, Major contribution are plannedfrom the following Colleges:-Humanities and Social Sciences, Geography Department.-Sciences, Geology Department.- Civiland Environmental Engineering, and Architectural Engineering,-Information Technology.- Collegeof Business and Economics, Statistics Department.Availablefacilities at these colleges (staff, computing, data, references, journals)provide the foundation for practical and research applications. A wide varietyof software for Remote Sensing and GIS applications is available in the UAEUniversity (see PART 3), with close relationships being maintained with vendorssuch as the ER MAPPER, ERDAS, Environmental Systems Research Institute (ESRI),AutoDesk, and Oracle.1.6 CAPACITY OF THEPROGRAMBased onthe currently available human resources in the Geography, Geology, Civilengineering and Information Technology departments that may participate .in theprogram (about 19 faculty members) and material facilities (fully equippedlaboratories), the coordinated program is capable of recruiting and graduatingapproximately 10 highly qualified students each year, leading to a long–term graduate student body of 50-60 students.1.7 AREA OF RESEARCH (POSSIBLERESEARCH TOPICS)Theresearch interests of the faculty members in the concerned departments span awide spectrum of topics in Remote Sensing, Geographic Information Systems andSatellite Positioning Systems theory and application. Areas of study include(but are not limited to):Naturalresources* Waterresource management (valleys, dams, groundwater, distribution)* Coastalstudies (oil spill detection, salinity, etc.)*Desertification creep monitoring* Landcover mapping*Environment (atmosphere, hydrosphere, biosphere, lithosphere pollutionmonitoring: air, soil, water, noise, etc.)*Agriculture (soil classification, crop production, yield management)* Forestmanagement information systems*Wildlife management* Geologyand hydro-geology (mineral and energy resources)* Digitalterrain modelingMan-madeapplications* Urbanand Regional planning (Land use mapping )*cadastral and Land Information Systems (LIS)*Automated Mapping and Facilities Management (AM/FM), utilities management(water, electricity, telephones, gas, etc.)*Surveying, mapping, and geodesy*Navigation application of GPS (Location Based Services)*Telecommunication*Transportation*Development and maintenance of geographic information systems*Web-based GIS and multi-media integration* Social,political, and legal issues related to remote sensing and GIS*Regional and global
